reviews.review_text:
**Recommended, but read this before playing Early Access**

A warning before you start playing the Early Access version: when the full game is released, your save will be wiped. That is the main reason why I am stopping after around 9 hours for now.

As for the game itself, it really clicks.

It is a very nice combination of ARPG and souls-like gameplay. Enemies are challenging and they can punish you very quickly if you just rush in and mash buttons without thinking. The combat feels satisfying, the graphics are honestly stunning, and the sound design and effects are polished. From a technical point of view, I really do not have much to complain about. Everything works and feels as it should.

That said, some mechanics and game loops still need rebalancing.

The biggest issue for me is resource gathering. I do not want to spend 30 minutes running around already visited areas collecting materials for potions or meals, only to get destroyed by a new boss in a few minutes and then repeat the whole gathering loop again. Either the resource amounts need to be higher, or the system needs to feel more rewarding and less like busywork.

Overall, the game has a lot of potential and already feels very solid. I will gladly come back to it, but probably after release, when I know my progress will actually stay.

Eyegasm graphics, the game looks gorgeous. The gameplay is fun and satisfying. I’m still waiting for the full release before giving my final review, but until then, if you need a game to occupy you for 50 hours or more, it’s an easy pick. You can try dozens of builds, and even after 70 hours of playtime, I still can’t decide which playstyle I truly want.

Quite an intriguing game with a palpable atmosphere conveyed through its fascinating art direction. Personally, I didn't have any issues with the camera, but sprinting and climbing bound to the same button sometimes caused my character to leap onto nearby objects instead of continuing to run. The gameplay is extremely punishing. Also, I absolutely despise RNG resource drops and random effects on crafted gear that can't be altered. This system felt more like a punishment and an unnecessary hindrance to character progression rather than an incentive to keep playing.

I'm still in the early game, exploring the hub, but it's excellent so far. Love the writing, atmosphere, visual style, music, controls, user interface, movement, combat. Looking forward to the full release! Oh, and you can pet the cat! :3

A very good game, i love the art style and the the feel of combat

the build variety is not as complex as id like but what is there is engaging, i'm a particular fan of the run in punch run out and then spend my focus taking out adds playstyle. not as much of a fan of the heavy playstyle, the slowness is fairly agitating. but when this game lets you go fast, i have a great time. 

on the topic of speed, this game punishes you HARD for going fast at times. a great deal of this game is platforming. the platforming in this game is not all that great, it's hard to feel out what jumps you can and cant make. that mixed with tying the climb/ parkor button to the sprint button makes for some frustrating moments. fall damage is 100% the biggest cause of most of my deaths in this game. which leads to me getting impatient, wanting to go fast, and then once again falling to my death.

another large portion of this game is collecting recourses to build out the main hub. this is by far my least favorite portion of the game. im sure for others they might enjoy going out picking flowers and chopping down trees to feel immersed, but for me it just feels like a road block in the way of my progression. on top of this, npc's have their wares and quests, not timed to in game time, but real life time. this is the part i find unacceptable. having to wait a real life 24 hours for gordon to refill his food supplies is infuriating. that plus the cost of build projects (20 iron bars, 20 tough leather, 15 saltstone, 15 spruce planks, and 5 linen) just for a level 2 shop upgrade is a HUGE time sink. i just want to swing my fists at some plague monsters.

overall i give it 7/10 so far. might be an 8 on full release if they fix some of the gameplay glitches. you might end up stuck in the terrain a fair bit.

It's an amazing souls-like Action Role-playing game. I really like that you have to manage your stamina resource and be tactical in combat. I also love the resource gathering system and building up the main town Sacrament to become a beautiful and bustling city. The game oozes a dark fantasy fairytale feeling and I love the boss designs in both visuals and gameplay.

The game has a lot of stuff I love to see! Good gameplay that has challenges that teach you how to play and grow your skills organically, a town that you get to upgrade over the game in meaningful ways, home building, multiplayer!

As of writing we're still early in the game but we love the art style and the world building, nothing feels like a grind to waste our time or pad out gameplay. I think durability as a system should be removed since it doesn't feel like it meaningfully adds to gameplay other than taking you out of whatever you're doing to pay a smith taxes.  
Enchanting and gem setting are great system that ensures even with the same items your builds can diverge pretty dramatically. The Bounty system changes up previous areas enough to be interesting to explore again. I feel like equipment weights are a bit on the high end, needing quite a bit of levels invested into equip load to have a loadout with any heavier armor, but i do like that strength and equip load are different stats so strength users aren't both the strongest DPS and most heavily armored by default.

The only issue my current group has is that it can be hard to keep track of your character in combat, an outline of your character or some sort of easy to see marker would be nice. It would also be nice to add an option in the menus for which screen the game is on, one player had an issue with it wanting to open on their secondary monitor.
Couldn't recommend this game more, one thing I'll mention to people thinking of buying the game is that progress will be lost/reset once the full game comes out.
Really fun so far the only reason I am not playing it anymore is because I want to experience the full game all at once when 1.0 releases, but it hurts to wait to play )=

I love this game! I can't wait for the official 1.0 release in October. I will absolutely be spending most of my time playing this game solo and with my husband. I'm so excited to dive back in with new content to play. I originally enjoyed watching my husband play the game, but decided to start playing it myself. I'm glad that I did!
Completed Early access, and damn what a game thoroughly enjoyed it. Will just do a lenghty review once the game gets released. Though the game right now is solid
It's been very long time since I played something this beautiful in both aesthetics and gameplay. It's a masterpiece in the making.

Started with a team of three, got to the endgame solo because everybody else left. I would say that there is a lot of good in this game, but all of it is undermined by junk in core game mechanics. The greatest offender here is enemy attack patterns. There are way to many attacks that are wrongly telegraphed to the player or impossible to read - large windups that are followed after arbitrary amount of time by a lightning fast strike, jump attacks that stop and hover in the air just for a few extra frames to mess with your parry windows, enemy spamming attacks without giving you much time to retaliate, enemy spamming teleports when you get close to them... It's so bad, that community consensus on the optimal playstyle is to play mage, stay as far away from enemies as possible and just blast them with spells before they can do anything to you. In other words - completly ignoring combat mechanics of the game. A simple visual and/or audio cue for attacks would've gone a long way in making the game playable for melee builds (which are the majority of weapon types btw).

Then there are MMO elements in the single player game. I'm not sure why this game needs grind for resources, daily quests, limited inventory space, long waiting times for resource refinement and so on. I've grinded here more then I've ever grinded in GW2, and that's an actual MMO.

Lastly artstyle. It's unique to say the least. Why most human characters look like that tube monster from Silent Hill 3, with their arms being unbelievably long and thick, and legs short and thin and long faces?. BBEG of this game had such a wide distance between her beady eyes and such a long face, that she look like a goat, which honestly never failed to make me laugh.

No rest for the Wicked is a good game with some rough edges. I really enjoyed my time but the game does not respect the players time. If you are employed, do not buy this game til release.

For more context, I have fully upgraded the town and I have max tier refineries and crafting tables. But I find myself constantly looking to farm crafting materials like this is some sort of MMORPG. The last reason I had to delete the game was that I farmed copper ores for more than 3 hours, and used those ores to craft a specific rapier that I like with a specific faucet. All those 3 hours went to waste, and you can not even get those mats back through dismantling the failed ones. So all in all, its a good game that wastes the players time through MMORPG loops.

I would not recommend you to buy until release.

This review was initially negative because I was having significant performance issues. Not sure what changed but Performance has improved significantly.
The game itself is a really satisfying mix mix of Arpg and somewhat soulslike. Gameplay feels great, hitting enemies has great weight to it.
Good variety of weapons and armor with the soulslike eguip load mechanics so you have to weigh your options, will you go with tanky armor with a higher level of protection or lighter build so you can have more agile movements. 
Some people might not like the idea of going back to previous areas to gather loot for crafting or upgrading your gear, but I like the incentive to go return to previous areas to grind a bit.
The enemies change so going through areas multiple times won't feel the same every time. Chests and loot replenish themselves.
Haven't played long enough to talk too much about the story so I'll update this review later on.
Great experience so far as long as the performance stays stable.

It’s been a long time since a game has surprised me this pleasantly; the feeling is very similar to what you experienced as a child when found across something truly interesting and extraordinary.

I’d especially like to highlight the visuals: the art style, the animations, and the tactile feel! it’s just amazing.

I recommend giving it a try, especially if you like action RPGs and isometric gameplay.

I love the combat in this game. Truly refreshing but the bosses can be tough. Its almost like they have infinite stamina and they are relentless in their attacks. So far, I am having a blast with the weapons and level design is peak. Love it

Great game, a few quality of life features missing, like tooltips for all the enhancements. Would be good if they released endless mode in crucible so you have a reason to grind for perfect gear. Cant wait for the full release to play it again.
10/10 great game, mix between Diablo and dark souls if you dig both of those games go for it

This is hands down the best game I've played this generation and this is coming from someone who doesn't usually play these types of games. Can't wait for the full version to come out! Hopefully soon!!

Pros-
-Pretty good hack and slash game with Base-upgrading elements as well as Character development giving the game not necessarily a replay value but longer Campaign time.

-Great visuals, intriguing story, daily- and weekly quests give you a reason as well to come back to the game even though you maxed-out your Character and Base.

-Rare bugs / getting soft-locked can be most of the time fixed by the "Travel to last Whisper"-option.

-You can fish! 10/10


Cons-
-FPS massive drop at some elevated places causing image-rendering problems. (At the Watcher i.e.)

-I wish that some weapons would be compatible with other attributes making a build with 3 different weapons more interesting. (gauntlets based solely on strength attributes i.e.)

-Some weapon-classes offer to few options. (i.e. Knives/ Daggers/ Maces)

-Plague seems to me to be a useless element.

A great buy at 30%~50% off. (That's because even a masterpiece videogame should not cost more than €/$ 35)

This is not a review, but a little ode of [i]praise and gratitude[/i].

The game is [b]an absolute marvel, [i]one in a thousand[/i][/b]. Ultimate visual and tactile delight. It's absorbed me from the first moments and just refuses to let go after many hours. Can't remeber last time I enjoyed exploration and just being in the world of a game this much. Cinematics are just astonishing, Charaters are vivid, memorable and devilishly charming. Art style is simply delicious. 

[b]A masterpiece without doubt[/b], desperately asking for prolonging and expansion.

Does it have anything to better and tune? Yes, but I'm somewhat blind to it right now in the light of the positive features of the game.



--==*^*==--
[i]P.S. Если Вы на Linux, и игра не подключается к онлайн мирам, запустите VPN в режиме TUN.[/i]
Great game. Great story. Great graphics. Great gameplay. Really looking forward to the 1.0 release this October!!!

Aside from the obvious optimisation issues and bugs that everyone is dealing with, this game is great so far. After 40-ish hours, a friend and I have pretty much exhausted everything to offer in its current state, excluding the dailies/weeklies and exalting everything. What I'm impressed with the most is the story, script and voice acting; it's written incredibly well and, it feels like what we have so far in early access is the entirety of Act I. SO WHEN IS 1.0 RELEASING?! It's set it up perfectly.

Faccinating world, interesting storylines, great characters developments. Needs some better optimisations though, it's yanky in the menu, which annoyingly the place I spent a lot of time in. Overal, a fantastic experience
Incredibly beautiful ARPG with amazing combat mechanics. Every swing feels like it has weight, the sound design is great, the voice acting is great, I just can't wait until it releases.

have played from the start, high care developers, beautiful game, always come back to it. amazing work. just logged in and can't believe how nice the ui is for multiplayer. 

starting out can be a slog but the game is very worth it. can't wait to see more

Beautiful game souls like combat punishing bosses upgradeable gear what's not to like. I enjoy that I can play for hours or for a few minutes and walk away to do life. Good story decent questing. Really easy to get in over your head in a hurry. PAY ATTENTION to danger level indicator it will save you humiliating defeats.

The art style in this game is very charming, in a grim, Souls-like way. The gameplay is the perfect mix between Elden Ring/Dark Souls and an ARPG. The world is an absolute masterpiece and crafted in a way that exploration feels extremely rewarding. You'll be climbing, finding all kinds of hidden areas (if you explore!) and constantly unlocking shortcuts here and there back to your "Whispers" (bonfires) just like in a souls-like.

Combat feels meaty and impactful, you can do some fun things with the gear for builds (although currently it's purely a stat-based system, the class system is being implemented in 1.0). Worth your hard-earned dollars for sure and something that I see having massive potential moving into the future. If you didn't tell me, I wouldn't know this game was still in Early Access.

As of  5/29, the next major update planned is 1.0, so if you're hesitant to try now then definitely join the discord and keep up with development. This is a true gem.

Overall 8.9/10.

Smoothest modern ARPG, visuals are insane, the hand painted look does wonders.
Huge hopes for the full release.
Fps drops too much in main town, fights are sometimes unfair as melee, but the game s too gorgeous to look at for that to matter much.
For an EA at this price, you can easily sink in 50 hours and not be bored.

Amazing and beautiful game. I love the map design the verticallity and the challanging combat. This game shows that izometric ARPG genre has much more to offer. The game is not easy but very satisfying

It's very different battle system and it takes a while to get into, but that is also a charm. Instead of smashing attack, you have to make it at the right time. Timing is a lot in No Rest, sometimes a bit to much, but I get the thought of why. 
It's a difficult game and I play on easy. I'm too old for high stake games. I would like either a custom setting or 5 difficult levels. Easy is slightly to easy but normal is too hard. 
The story and world is probably one of the best I've played. 

Highly recommended but it's complicated and a very different battle system so give it some time.
Odporúčam, skvelý systém súbojov. Snáď sa dočkáme pokračovania príbehu a dlhého.

So as people already mentioned, this game is beautiful! And it really hit the nail on building something new and exciting. The aesthetics and feel you get when you walk around the world is truly remarkable.
The combat is soulslike: Punishing, and rewarding when you master it. Theres good variety of monsters and attacks to master.
So in these 2 senses moon studios really knocked it out of the park.
Where this game still struggles:
1. The resource harvesting system, is not fun. While chopping trees, picking up mushrooms or fishing has its moments of peace, the amount of time you spend doing it, is way too much, and still it's easy to run out of resources.
2. The RPG element of this game is still severly lacking (I know they are working on a class system). But at the current time, it's very unrewarding. On the flipside you would think that it would make it easy to transition between different styles and weapons. But no, a respec is required every time, really limiting experimentation and wasting resources.
3. The ARPG element of this game is also very lacking:
 * This game doesn't have any good endgame mechanic or extra progression after finishing the main quest.
 * I found the system for grinding for good gear, pretty bad. It's not the worst, but the amount of resources it takes to craft the sword or armour you want, is pretty immense. For a lot of these resources, there's no clear path to get them. For example I wanted to craft a Mayhem Greatsword, this requires Bear Hearts. There are no real ways to get them consistantly, so after a few failed crafts I'm either forced to change my playstyle or just accept my current build.
 * Build varietly is given to you by abillities in the way of runes, the system feels like it needs more. Runes by themselves don't feel unique enough to justify picking one over the other in any meaningful way to make a build. Also experimenting with different runes like builds is not very accessible, and the game makes no effort to push you to experiment. For example thers a rune attack "double crush" and a rune attack "triple crush", why pick one over the other? Style points?

So far I really love the game and would recommend it to others. Now here are some things I like, dislike, or have suggestions for.

GOOD THINGS:
Gear Enchanting:
I enjoy the enchanting of gear. Even at low levels there are enchantment boosts like 20% to something and that feels good. There are items to put enchantments onto gear and you can use that to override current enchantment to reroll that single enchantment. Another item can rip enchantments off to be put on others. These are all amazing! Granted I wish they dropped more often. 
Levels:
I can feel the impact when I level up my stats. Putting into vitality I can survive more hits right away. I can feel the impact of more stamina just putting 3 points into the stat. It does take a bit to level after getting around 7ish. But if 30 is the max level then I understand. I'm level 11 after 9.7 hours.
Visuals:
It's a very stylized game and it looks great! It also runs great while looking like it's all painted. Some small visual glitches. I've especially noticed the daily and weekly board icons blip in and out on my AMD GPU. But nothing game breaking so far. 


BAD:
Crash:
I did experience one crash while fast traveling. But the game auto saves often and I didn't lose anything. Also it's in beta I expect some crashes here and there. It's only 1 crash in 10 hours of play. 
Crafting:
I like the idea of crafting. I went to make a shield that said the requirements to use where 10 strength. I made shield and the requirement was 22 strength after crafted. I went and gathered and refine materials for an hour and then found out the requirements to use I have no points in. Did not feel good. 
Parry:
My brother was also playing with me some after 9 hours. I had played solo up to that point. I didn't realize there was friendly fire and killed him a few times on accident. He's a hardcore souls fan but did not like the game the first hour. He says the parry window is too small and feels off to him. He also was having problems with stamina management but he never got past 3rd level. I think he just needs more time to level and put into stamina and vitality. But IDK if the parry window can be changed with gear or not. I personally quite parrying and just used a big shield.

SO SO STUFF:
Exploration:
You will need to explore and discover the map. It's not going to hold your hand. I got lost in the first area for like 1.5 hours and had gone back and forth like 5 times looking for the access to the fort. It was up a slop and a hole in the wall and I had thought that slop was a small ledge that couldn't be climbed. I ran right by it so many times until I studied the map and there wasn't a line right there. But that made me find a bunch of gear and leveled a couple times before I found it. Running back to a bonfire or to the city can feel tedious and I end up killing myself to get back to a bonfire quickly and just pay to have gear repaired.
Update: I found out you can get a rune that lets you fast travel to any 'bonfire' as long as it is unlocked. You can be anywhere to use it. Makes it way more tolerable then just killing yourself when you get too far away from city or 'bonfire'.
Gathering:
I'm not opposed to gathering. But I've done like 4 outing out the first main city to just chop wood to try progress the city amenities. I really feel like I should get extra materials if I hit a perfect gather. I know it makes me gather faster if I hit it, but I'd like a bonus material if I consistently hit perfect. 
Edit: I'm dumb I'm getting the resources doubled from the perfect gathers. I think the amount of resources should be tuned up a little or make the amenities in the city a little less for the first upgrades. 
Research:
I like the researching being a thing to be able to craft things. I'm ok with the random weapons unlocked to craft, but I would really like it if armors you unlocked in sets. Just make the armor spinner cost more research and give me the whole armor set. I like to cosmetic hunt. I want the matching helmet, chest, leggings, and gloves. Also I can see this taking forever to unlock all the research if it continues at the pace it has been.
Character creation:
I would like it if I could having a bit of a body slider. To make my character either skinner or bigger. Buffer or fatter. Taller or shorter. I think that would make the character creator way better.
Game is fantastic. They definitely need more content though. Everyone complaining about friendly fire is just bad at video games

This game is amazing! The world is super fun to explore and the graphics and aesthetics are perfect. The co-op though is where this game really shines. The friendly fire makes this game much more strategic. And the more you play with friends, the more you find yourself bobbing and weaving with your friends to get out of the way of their attacks and kite other creatures. It's so satisfying. I'd definitely recommend.

This game is SO GOOD. It is the perfect mix of Diablo style character building with Elden ring style combat and the art is tremendous. I just realized this is in Early Access and that makes me so excited for the future of this game. It is nearly perfect. All the proposed updates sound awesome, but all I want is more story quests and more map areas. Maybe a big ask but this game is just so fun that I WANT MORE!

Disclaimer: I played this game strictly solo so I wont be speaking on the multiplayer aspect at all which I understand is a big part of the game but all my friends are on console and I'm shy so solo it was.

TLDR: This game is a genuinely fun and engaging initial playthrough experience where the only shortcomings are the lack of endgame content and build options. It feels special and has a lot of potential to become something truly great 

Pros: This game is mechanically very fun. Combat is without a doubt the star of the show with weapons having their own special attack patterns. Mix in the use of a multitude of spells and abilities plus dodging, blocking and parrying and you get a combination that really doesn't get old. Fights are weighty and impactful (especially in the beginning). I really liked the departure it took from other like top down games like Diablo or POE where the fights are more dark souls-esque and more intimate instead of just slaughtering hordes of forgettable enemies all the time. The characters had personality and the world felt full. The voice actors brought life to their characters and made me invested in the plights. The sound design is exceptional. That crack that "fire burst" makes when you hit the sweet spot evaporating enemies and leaving only scorched earth behind is addicting. There's a good variation between enemies as you progress through the story and many are region specific which adds to the identity of the different areas. The map is scenic and I'm a fan of the unique art style. Hidden paths combined with the labyrinth style level design with moveable platforms and puzzles sprinkled in made going through the story a blast! I honestly couldn't wait to get home from work to continue where I left off.

Second Disclaimer: The game is still in early access so I'm giving grace and honestly if you are happy with having a very solid around 50-60 hr experience don't worry too much about the cons I'm about to list

Cons: 

Armor has no real identity
There are 4 different armor types: cloth, leather, mesh and plate which are only differentiated by appearance, resistance rating and weight. The enchantments are random rolled and interchangeable between all the material types meaning there is really no need to carefully choose which armor type to use. There are no for instance "exclusive to cloth" enchantments so it boils down do if you are good at dodging and parrying do whatever but if not go heavy so that even when you get smacked around a lot you don't die.

Enchantments are weak and generic
None of the enchantments I have come across in my 72 hours of playing feel like they have any impact on build or playstyle. The enchantments are pretty much all very generic. Complete X action (dodge damage, deal damage or kill) to get damage increase for however many seconds or decrease stamina usage, etc. The enchantments really just make the game easier but they don't really add anything of significance to your experience other than that. There is no "you are surrounded by an icy aura that inflicts freeze on anything within 5m" or "5% chance to resurrect the last killed enemy to fight for you for 15 seconds" enchantments to play into your personal power fantasy.

The only way to improve the enchantments on your gear is very expensive resource wise and the increase is typically negligible even in endgame. For instance I am at 4 out of a max 5% fire damage on my chest armor. In order to exalt my gear (and POTENTIALLY get that number to 10%) I need to first max this stat but it will take multiple radiant embers to do so. Enchantments seem to be increased on a percentage basis and when you originally roll you land somewhere between 0 and 100 percent bonus for that enchantment. Say I decide to strengthen my enchantment so I use radiant ember to get to 5%. The game does not go straight from 4 to 5, instead it does something like 4 to 4.25 to 4.5 to 4.75 to 5. I can play for 6 hours and find maybe 1 radiant ember but knowing that regardless of whether I use it or not, I'll still be at 4% after the fact keeps me from bothering with it in the first place.

Legendary weapons and rings are boring
Pretty much the same situation as with the enchantments section. The gold equipment, if nothing else, was where they could do something fun with the builds but they stuck to pretty much the same extra damage or extra resistance schtick. Pretty unremarkable which made me not care to grind crucible for them.

I played this with one other person. At first I was lost since I am used to games holding my hand, but this one respects the player and actually challenges you. Beautiful art style, lovely voice acting and writing, and an interesting story. The gameplay mechanics are simple yet fun and intriguing, and there are tons of ways to build your character. However, the mountain of grind required to put a build together forces the average player to commit to just one, with no real room to explore others. This is my only gripe, devs, you perfected everything else, then gate kept players from the fun of exploring builds alongside the story. The endgame is decent, with a roguelite mode. As it stands, it is worth the price.

A decent souls-like with a nice art style and enjoyable combat. The movement is clunky and there's not enough content as of yet. Also don't like that you have to think hard to progress with the main quest sometimes - finding secrets is okay for extra loot but don't gate the story progression with it.

There are so many good reasons to like this game. It's challenging, but fair. The art directions is top tier. The story is compelling. But my favorite thing about this game is that it only takes like 15 seconds to get into the game from launching. 

Currently the only thing I would like to see changed is for resources to automatically enter your inventory after mining, digging or chopping.

I don't like those type of games... BUT
I do enjoy playing this game after grinding on more intense games like Marathon, where in this game, No rest for the wicked, you have downtime in a beautiful environment very colorful and Dark at the same time.

Not used to game like this one, it took me a lot of time understanding the gear system that is very versatile, a note on that, I feel that the gears could see an upgrade in aesthetic. Not a huge fan. Not bad either, just not great.

The world seems kind of small until you realise that even the first spawn has so much hidden path that leads to new places.

The game as you probable read already is very punishing in a way that you will die again and again, but that is fine, compare to an extraction shooter.. you will not loose your items and that feels good, when you don't want to fight and just gather resources it becomes almost peaceful and I love that little time going around, the game spawn a few enemies just to keep you busy in between resources but is not pushing too hard on the fights. when you don't want to. The challenges and bounty are nicely done in my opinion. 

Interestingly there's a rogue-lite mode that don't gives XP but item/item upgrades for the progression, I think this is a nice touch added to the game. And if not mistaken, is part of the story. 

What I would like to see in the future, is having the Focus going back to full while in Sacrament, the stamina locked too for that area.. Or is that part of the sleeping things? Don't know don't have a bed yet :)

Overall... Good game that surprised me, a non souls-like player

--SPOILER FREE--

For an early access game, it's worth every penny.

-The graphics are some of the best I've seen in a long, LONG time

-The gameplay is engaging, challenging without being grindy, and never gets boring or repetitive

-The story isn't convoluted for the sake of being "mysterious". It's not difficult to understand the main beats

-The world feels alive, as if you were only one part of it, instead of being the center

-The voice acting is phenomenal, especially the voice actor of Lara (those who know get it)

The game was clearly made with love from the beginning foundations. You won't find any forced political agendas, forced tediousness or horrible dialogue here. The co-op mode, along with the semi-survival base builder mechanics bring it to the next level, as you visibly see the city grow along with you, the player.

Buy this game if you like an interesting dark and disturbing world, mixed with disturbing body horror, gore and endless weapons, animations and unique enemies.

Love it. Definitely recommend it.
I love and hate this game, but either way, I am definitely addicted to it...
please change the boss in the brine vault it is overwhelmingly unfair. Smaller boss for smaller space please.

Note : I played it in coop with 2 people. 
It's an ok game trying to do too much at the same time and thus it delivers half baked mecanics. The art direction is perfect, the combat gameplay is good but not great, the time gated mecanics are stupid and pointless. No Rest could be a lovely game with more quality of life improvements and less player time wasted.

This would be a great souls-like if it weren't isometric. The art and combat are nice but having your camera locked in place for a game this focused on tactical combat is counter-intuitive. Overall recommend but this game could've been so much better. 6/10

Such a beautifully crafted game.
Every scene feels like a painting brought to life, and the atmosphere pulls you completely into its world. It’s one of those rare games where the visuals, music, and gameplay blend together perfectly to create something genuinely memorable

Highly recommended, game is in great state already - 33h with no problems.
Engaging, lots of content, well balanced all around.

Beautiful as we know from the Orio makers.

Duo-Co-Op worked very well, looking forward to full release!

TL;DR: 9/10 for Dark Souls(tm) fans; 6/10 for everyone else.

I want to begin by saying I personally adore this game, but it is not without frustrations. I can lose a whole day trying and trying to complete roguelike ‘crucible’ runs without success and give up after a short while to the main story where a difficult boss encounter blocks progress, and feel joy when the boss finally falls (being truthful, I have not yet beaten the crucible, but hope to change that soon).

If you’re easily frustrated, the cozy elements of upgrading your main base will not make up for the punishing combat. I have wanted to rage quit as often as I have celebrated a challenging boss’ defeat. 

I miss some aspects of MMO gaming, and this game slakes those cravings. You can complete daily and weekly quests within a ‘realm’ environment comprising of 3 other players wherein you can contribute materials and weapons for the good of your group of friends. While not a wide net, your group can cultivate and curate an environment unique to your playgroup. 

The atmosphere is one of the best I have experienced- at the cost of the main story being soft locked behind a difficultly paywall. While rewarding, I have to concede Elden Ring’s game system allow for more immediate boss fight restarts.

My biggest gripes are that in the roguelike ‘Crucible’ mode, you are locked at 1 attempt unless you unlock an ability that resurrects you, and the resurrections don’t always work. If you play multiplayer and die, your teammates cannot revive you, even if they succeed in completing the level. I wish so badly for a change where maybe your teammate(s) can revive you at the end of the round or at the cost of their active health. 

Combat is smooth despite the punishing elements, and I can often see where I made a mistake to try and avoid it in the future. Diversity of effective builds seems high. Above all else, you can work together with your friends to upgrade your town and feel well-earned progression rewards. The environment changes with your growth and it makes the world feel very rich and alive.

It's a very good game, with the kind of pacing that I think I wanted. Not as much Dark Souls in which even the most trivial of enemies can slice you in two, but also not like the other tradition of ARPGs descendants from the Diablo mechanics, like Grim Dawn, which are excellent games, but offer a completely different approach to dealing with enemies.

In No Rest for the Wicked it's a nice balance of having to approach each enemy with caution, but even big bosses have a timing and a rhythm to them that you can either parry or dodge and counter. Every enemy can teach you something new, and deaths, at least with the current mechanics, aren't really punishing, as they just degrade your weapons. Have a spare realm saved at the beginning at hand that you can quickly hop in and repair your equip for free, and you can throw as many deaths as it's fun to play through onto any enemy.

All in all I found No Rest for the Wicked pacing requiring you to slow down a little and appreciate the game for its gorgeously crafted scenarios and superb animations, with every tree I cut down a reminder to slow down a little to appreciate this thing.

I hope their 1.0 update would polish the heres and theres in Sacramento that break the illusion a bit and betray this is still in early access. Very excited to play when 1.0 hits nonetheless!

Grab on sale and then play at full release. The core experience feels very good and polished. They just need to cook up more of it.

Performance is an issue that needs attention before final release. It was playable, but heavy frame drops do happen in some areas more than others.
Good game but the most dangerous enemy in the game is all the ledges. PLEASE do something like giving us a jump button instead of a 50/50 kill myself

Gorgeous game. However, for what I want in this game the control mechanics are to janky and slow and leans too much into souls like. Would have liked a bit more tightness in controls. everything else is pretty much good. 7-8/10

This game's really good and the "grind" cries are pretty overblown. The tradeoff is "either you buy everything from the shop with gold" or you can "acquire resources" while you're playing the game to get stuff normally. I have plenty of resources simply from playing the game, clearing stuff where I am like normal. I've done maybe 2 "resource loops" ever, and it's fine anyway because you clear an area of enemies, snag the resources you want, and then just leave. I really fail to understand the problem. You're either going to have to "grind gold" like every other game, or, in this game, you can "get a few resources" (usually while doing a quest or a bounty), and then fully customize an item, down to its exact enchantments and everything. 

Okay, that out of the way:
Really nice little ARPG. Makes me think of something like Kingdoms of Amalur meets Fable. Fantastic art, fantastic music, fantastic VA. Plays responsively. Performance has hitches here and there; there are some areas where frame drops are noticeable, but they are likely "loading" areas because the game is otherwise completely interconnected and you can go virtually anywhere in real time, so I imagine this has certain sacrifices. Hopefully the loading maps or what not could be further optimized / made more seamless before 1.0.

Don't really have much more to say yet! I could quibble about a plethora of "black" characters with Irish accents (this is odd and has to be done on purpose, there are many different accents at play in this game and I'm not sure every accent has to be European) but who cares at this point. Gaming is what it is, this kinda thing isn't going anyway any time soon.

I've been enjoying the story and the game play the entire time. The character "building" at this moment in time is somewhat shallow; admittedly, but there are changes coming including some kind of "jobs" system. I've not tried multiplayer yet, not sure how it feels or scales or performs with multiple players. Combat is fun, pretty exciting, reasonably fair. Seems to me that there's no inherently "weak" character types, it all comes down to player execution. In this way, at the moment, character building is much like Dark Souls. All you really have control over are some stats, and your weight load. Do what you want to do! Good enough, but I'd like to see something like Grim Dawn's class and/or constellation system to really put a "finishing touch" on any particular character you're playing as. 

Anyway, I'd say great game so far, can't wait to see what 1.0 looks like! I've been flabbergasted by some of the vistas and encounters, and it has been just plain fun playing through. Definitely gets a recommend!

Absolutely gorgeous game with a very interesting story, very fun to engage in combat as well as the side activities like house decor and mat farming, I agree with the criticism about some parts of it being excessively grindy though. I hope we get to see the official release addressing some of these issues. Please give us more closed-off armet helmets and make parrying useful, I beg you

This is so good. It has a little bit of everything. You can work on projects in the town, tons of weapons and armour, really good graphics and stylization, and so much more that would take me too long to list.

This is easily a Yes, let me break it down for you:

Pros:
+ Good graphics
+ Fluid animation
+ Surprisingly well crafted cinematics
+ Good souls-like gameplay. If you played any souls like, you will feel at home. Though the game is not as challenging as any souls game, it still provides a satisfying challenge on "Normal" difficulty.
+ There are difficulty options, so if you like rewarding hardcore gameplay, feel free to tune up the difficulty.
+ Map design is superb. There are clear distinct biomes, and within the biomes it is separated into lots of areas that are crafted with care, along with masterful use of elevation. The game rewards exploring too. There are lots of optional and hidden paths and will certainly surprise you even when you have completed the game.
+ There is town building so you have meaningful things to do aside from combat.
+ Good looting and gear system with fixed stats but RNG abilities like diablo. There are different builds to try.
+ Lots of different weapons with unique animations.

Neutral:
= Gravity will kill you a lot. During exploration, you will be misled by some camera angle/distance/terrain, and will fall a lot to your death. Cheap deaths are a theme of souls like but it feels more "unfair" here, so putting this as neutral
= The scaling near late game is not as fun as early game. Possible skill issue, but late game even with level matching gear and correct stat scaling, I feel like it demand way more hits on enemies to kill them. At the same time, wearing medium armour still pretty much means nothing and I still get killed in 2-3 hits. So you either go for light gear or extremely heavy gear. I feel like it needs some tuning.
= Sometimes the map design can be frustrating. Yes they make good maps. But sometimes the camera angle will cause you to overlook some clues or objects, that you will end up wasting time looping around looking for the way forward. Not essentially a problem, just annoying at times.

Cons:
- Not a fan of the camera. This game uses a fixed isometric camera angle. It will block your view at times especially when fighting large bosses. With a game that demands flawless reaction it felt cheap to lose to the camera.
- Resource gathering is tedious. You have the option to rebuild the town in order to gain better "services" and goods. And they require alot of resources. Even if you chop down every tree you see and mine every ore you come across, you will still be short quite a lot. I had to make dedicated farming runs for stuff. Really time wasting and a deliberate move to drag gameplay.
- Another thing that drags gameplay is the daily and weekly missions. You can do these optional quests to earn money and free gear. However, they follow a real time schedule (yes you can tune your windows clock to fool it) and it plays like an MMO. This design is not appropriate and should be revamped.
- This brings on the next problem. Your character starts out with limited inventory space and gear slots. And you have to get an item to expand them. This item drops from bosses and from enemies in weekly missions. I really don't see the point of this limited inventory design. This brings inconvenience rather than meaningful difficult gameplay. It forces you to go back to town often to store your stuff in chests before you can venture out again. Breaking the flow of gameplay. Another attempt to drag the game out. People are making new worlds to farm bosses + weekly missions to get this item too. When this happens you know your design is bad because people cannot satisfy it from normal gameplay.
- Performance is borderline okay. Sometimes loading times get long the later I am into the game, and some random DPS dips as well. Surprisingly demanding for a game like this.
- Seems to have controller bug that the X button (or A button or xbox) will sometimes mis-register and double tap.


All in all, this is still a very solid game and will definitely entertain you for dozens of hours. Do note that this game is still EA and the story is not finished. There are no achievements yet so you might want to hold out until 1.0 release (which might happen in 2027 or 2028). However if you don't mind the above then definitely get this game and venture on a rewarding journey!

This is a really exciting game and fits such a great niche for souls-likes with a more mmo type feel. I have played enough to know that I am definitely going to sink a lot of time in, and just want to wait for the 1.0 release at the moment.

Key positives:
- Difficulty on par with the souls franchise (depending on your pace), really rewarding combat
- A very cool build crafting system with lots of room for expression
- Beautiful graphics and art style
- The world has that ambience, so it's fun even just roaming without an objective

A few negatives:
 - Still needs more content, expecting this to change closer to 1.0
- Having limited inventory space is a super annoying mechanic, you can expand it through Ichor farming but it sucks to play inefficiently and leave behind resources initially.
- The food/crafting/house building can be quite a lot of grinding. It is only slightly too much for me at the moment but may get easier with more consistent play.
I enjoy all of it so far. As others say, it is a little grindy but I recommend it.

Love the game as it is a lot of fun. Only true negative, at least as a keyboard user, is movement. It's horrendous without a controller. Whether it be platforming or combat, you will randomly jump off a cliff, or double jump for no reason off a cliff, or slide off the cliff, or stand next to a guy that will push you off a cliff, or climb to the top of a cliff, to then randomly fall off a cliff. Other than that, its an amazing game.

For an early access game, this work in progress is a truly wonderful playthrough. While only playing in Act 1, or the first 1/3rd of what the game will be, I was drawn in so deeply that I played thirty hours in the span of one week! By no means is this game ground-breaking or seem to have any incredibly unique feature to define the title. Instead, the game designers were able to pull off combining all great ingredients from different genres and adding them up to make a fantastic new stew! A different type of Souls-like, with map pathing similar to Jedi Fallen Order, interesting runes similar to your Sheika Slate in Breath of the Wild, and a fair amount of crafting and survival. The combat is fantastic, both incredibly challenging and hugely rewarding! There were multiple times and specific locations in the game that took me multiple, multiple tries to survive and clear the mobs/boss, but when I finally did I would sometimes let out huge sighs of relief or the occasional cheer! I do hope that the skill point system is altered to be more impactful to gameplay, but the game is far from finished, so I have lots of hope. Sound design is wonderful, the artwork was genuinely breathtaking at times, and the story-telling actually felt compelling in an age of bland, overused stories. The 30+ hours I've spent playing No Rest for the Wicked were hours well spent. I intend to return to the game and try a new character from scratch!
Combat: 9/10
Story: 10/10
Graphics and Artwork: 9/10
Audio Design: 9/10

OVERALL I give it 9.5/10

Amazing graphics and feeling in 3D Isometric space, map depth and elevation even aerial slam attacks from higher positions are fun. The combat is satisfying however it is lacking build diversity and feels more like your being guided down a narrow pathway. 

The biggest issue is the auto aiming and spells and abilities not hitting, the game also has an auto jumping mechanic if your sprinting which can be really frustrating at times. So being designed for consoles will have bigger community out reach but at the cost of fluency. 

Being in early access I can say that it can only go up, I have enjoyed my time playing so far and with the addition of arena and an extended level cap I can't see me stopping anytime soon.
Great combat and fantastic level design ! Really hope to see more of the main story line when the game releases

I have 50 hours now on 2 save files and im really impressed. This Game combines a top down perspektive with soulslike combat (which i personally am a really big friend of). 
Thats good and nice and all but not that "exclusive".

What really catched me is the City-Building factor. It is so amazing that i can now play this game, but not always have to fight. I can relax and farm materials for the city or gather food for health items. The Daily/Weekly Quest system is also really good.

Breakdown:
Pro: - nice smooth combat
- no camera issue (souls gamer will now what i mean)
- many different weapons and builds (interesting system)
- CITY BUILDING!!!
- Multiplayer (very smooth)
- overall artstyle, beautifull

Cons:
- I think some buiilds need a bit of adjustement. Rapier fells kinda weak. (maybe i need to play it more so dont take it to serius)
- Friendly fire (My friend plays with a big bonk club, which always hits me when i try to help him in combat. i think its kinda annoying that you cant have a smooth fight that way, cause i always have to respect his range)


Summary: A wonderfull game, needs some minor adjustements but im really looking forward to the future of the game and am very optimistic that it is going to be a masterpiece when its full release comes. It is already close enough. 
Love you and will support you in any way i can. 
Keep it up!!!!

It's probably one of the most interesting top down action RPGs I've played so far. Feel like it borrows a bit from Dark Souls and Path of Exile, along with some surprisingly fun world traversal. Each weapon has it's own moveset, for better or worse, along with the ability to slot 4 special skills to really customize how you fight. Enemy design could be more interesting, but they def get more advanced by the time you get to Marin Woods.

My biggest complaints are: that it leaves off at maybe 40% of the full game from what I can tell, some weapon types have far too few choices, and equipment load is a painful stat to invest in, with over 20 needed just to wear a few pieces of "mesh" weight gear.

Overall, very excited for 1.0. Will still be playing the multiplayer with friends for a bit before then too.

THIS GAME IS INCOMPLETE AND PROGRESSION WILL BE WIPED BEFORE RELEASE!!!!

Beautiful, fun game. I have a lot of fun with the combat system and building up the main hub. Combat and "platforming" can get a bit fiddly and annoying at times. Also a bit grindy. Controller recommended.
Very unique and fun, cant wait for 1.0 version of it.

I know this game is in early access but it genuinely is a must buy. Myself and a buddy played twelve hours straight the other day and still have barely scratched the surface and can't wait to get back in. Give this game a chance because if it continues to develop in the direction it is headed the game will be simply excellent. Combat story and graphics are ALL something new and interesting. 100% recommend.
Great production quality, Super fun and repeatably playable mechanics, and so much story and secret exploration!
Difficult but insanely fun, especially with a friend.

Tentative recommend.
Fun gameplay, unique fusions of different genres, and beautiful game world and graphics.

Issues/things to be aware of:
Weird periodic performance drops and issues, possibly due to optimization. Kinda grindy in some ways, but not in a fun or normal grindy way, more like a "this upgrade requires you to get tons of resources and should scale based on how many players are in the guild/world" kind of grindy. Think like how Warframe does guild grinding and how it scales to the size of the guild, this game needs that functionality for the town upgrade costs.
Some armor is just stupidly heavy for zero reason and it's difficult to reasonably wear. (nitpick)
Movement controls are janky, but tolerable for me as a veteran of both diablo-like arpgs and soulslikes, but many will find it frustrating and too clunky.
The roguelite dungeon mode is too long for what it offers, especially as a distraction from the main mode. The boss is also just outright unfair if you go in underleveled. Should really just scale to level when you first find it or have a periodic level increase since there's different difficulties anyways. Lock the related quest to finding it behind a level minimum so people don't waste time trying it on a character that's never going to be able to handle it adequately.
Beautiful game. Exploring the world is fun and the combat is challenging. Animations are world class
Its a fantastic game, but at this stage in development I reckon its ♥♥♥♥ or get off the pot when it comes to releasing the full title.
2 Crashes in 2 Hrs. For the price, that is too much crash.  Also, the material grind is a 10x too much. (Deal breaker, I had to mod the game to have fun)  Otherwise a really good game.

Once you get past the first hour of figuring out the mechanics the game is fun. The only thing that doesn't make sense is the fact that once you find a fast travel location you basically have to buy it to use it. Idk what the dev was thinking but he did not cook.

Another thing that absolutely sucks about this game is all the farming. Chopping down 100 trees and mining 100 ores just to get some upgrades isn't fun, not to mention you basically have to do a mini game to do these, then individually pick up every piece.

All in all, the gameplay seems fun for the most part

This game is special and deserves recognition. It wins on so many fronts - Art style and design, story, gameplay, voice acting....the list goes on. I would recommend this game to anyone and everyone, my only lament is that it took me this long to find and maybe that I can't play 1.0 yet! please go play this game and support this studio, gems like this are rare.
Enjoyable and quite polished game for early access. Recommend you get it on sale.

Downloaded this game in April of 2026, big update rolls out the second day we're playing it- 2 years after early access release. It seems like the devs keep pouring into this game and it really shows. Lot's of fun with friends but also a bit of an undertaking. Plenty of depth, fun exploration, and challenging gameplay. Can't believe this hasn't gotten more attention. Highly recommend for co-op fun

Fantastic game, slight issues with camera placement in boss battles but that's probably a skill diff. 
Wish the movement for k+m was better in target selection/directional movement but love everything else about the game.

I want to rate this game positively, but since I played it in co-op with my partner, the experience has been horrible. The game keeps disconnecting, and we constantly have to fight over loot and resources. Clearly, the devs implemented co-op as just an extra feature and put zero thought into balancing it. I’ll change my review if things improve in the future.

funny long arms in character creation
great gameplay
awesome cutscenes
interesting world building

First, the things I didn’t enjoy that much:

Everyone praises the art style, but personally these character designs don’t really click with me. It’s hard to find an armor set that makes you feel like a complete badass (although the gameplay helps with that later).
There’s a bit too much material grinding. Early on it feels natural, but later it starts becoming tedious.
I know it’s still Early Access, but the story hasn’t fully grabbed me yet. I’m usually the type of player who spends hours watching lore videos for games like Elden Ring, Cyberpunk, Expedition 33, or Inscryption.
The music is decent, but I expected more from it.

Now the great part, and the reason I recommend this game:

If you like soulslikes, flashy moves, impactful combat, and satisfying sound effects, this game can absolutely give you that. Even though I complained about a few things, I’ve still been playing a lot of No Rest for the Wicked because of the gameplay.

Throwing an axe that triggers a thunder effect, hearing enemies explode… yeah, that stuff feels awesome.

Haven’t tried co-op with friends yet, but that’s definitely my next step.

Pros:
The overall presentation of this game is fantastic. Moving through the world at various times of day and weather, it always looks/sounds/feels immaculate. It took some getting used to that you and most NPCs are shaped like homunculi for whatever reason, but I started engaging with the combat and world systems I felt immediately hooked. The bosses are challenging, combat feels responsive and rewarding most of the time. The roguelike mode keeps things pretty diversified in terms of grinding for gear and XP so you don't typically feel like you're doing the same thing over and over. I haven't been able to engage with the co-op aspects, none of my friends have gotten the game yet, but if they ever decide to pick it up I will be right there for another run. Love to see where this game goes. 

Cons:
This game needs shaders for armor to make them more uniform with each other. I am always wearing mismatched sets and it kinda makes me look like a clown sometimes. Resource gathering and upgrading vendors is extremely tedious and once you have upgraded them to level 2, they are still selling worse gear than is already in your inventory. You should start getting larger quantities of resources like copper and pine wood in later levels, like resource gathering spots that give you significantly more than 2 or upgraded tools should collect higher quantities of lower level resources. It does harm the flow of the game to stop what you're doing regularly and go back to the earlier areas to grind for resources for 30 minutes to an hour just so you can upgrade the town alongside your game progression.

A great game! As an ex wow player i can see myself engaging with this game for years to come. With so much scope for builds you can create something that works for you and because its not an MMO there's no pressure the keep up with meta and you can experiment as much or as little as you want without feeling like youre falling behind!
Super fun game, could get lost in playing it for hours with friends or even by myself. For sure going to keep exploring the game as every turn something new and exciting is waiting.
Fun when it works, Doesn't connect to online 90% of the time and have to restart game 8 times or restart PC to get it to force a reconnect.

Me start game. Me go river. Me want fish.

Me try catch fish. But bar thingy appear next to ME. Why?? Bar should stay by water where fish live. Me no fish, me just stand there confused. Bar follow me like angry bee.

Me no want adventure. Me no want puzzle. ME WANT FISH.

Fix please. Caveman tired.

P.D: The rest of the game is great, recommended.

I bought this game ages ago and let it sit in my library, but after finally diving in this week, I’m kicking myself for waiting so long.

The art style, fluid movement, and combat system are incredibly unique—it’s a breath of fresh air in a genre that can sometimes feel repetitive. I’ve played my fair share of ARPGs, but most of them focus entirely on the "loot explosion" high. This game is different. It actually forces you to engage; whether it’s figuring out a difficult boss pattern or solving environmental puzzles to cross a cliffside, it keeps you thinking.

I’ve intentionally avoided trailers to keep the experience pure, and it’s paying off. I’m having a blast and can’t wait to see where the developers take this next. Highly recommended.

Best souls-like I've ever played, and I've played plenty. It actually innovates on the souls systems rather than just mimic them. Although it's supposedly "Early Access" it's actually very well polished, and has many fleshed out systems (crafting, upgrades, enchanting, runes, etc.). The multiplayer is super fun too as the game is still challenging with friends but not too hard. The art style and cutscenes are gorgeous and the story is interesting although a bit slow. Combat is extremely satisfying even when fighting weaker enemies.

The camera can be awkward sometimes and there can be some sensory overload in certain locations, and the HUD is unfortunately too small and moves with your character which can be hard to keep track of. but these are some minor downsides in an otherwise incredible game.

Although I typically don't try early access games because I want to experience the full, best version of games, I was wanting to try this for a while because it looked promising and Moon Studio has such a great track record with Ori. I was very pleasantly surprised by this game once I played it.

It has engaging combat that can be pretty challenging, tons of room for character growth with different gears and builds, utterly incredible art that contrasts striking character designs and a gorgeous world with enchantingly grotesque monsters, and excellent levels that loop back and on top of themselves in fantastic ways. The narrative really engaged me as well, and the gradual build-up of the main city alongside daily and weekly bounties helped flesh the game out.

It seemed like a remarkably full package, right up until the story just stopped after a big, exciting reveal that left me hurting to know and experience more. Talk about a cliff hanger! I can't blame an early access game for not being done, of course, but the cut-off was definitely sharp.

A couple things I wasn't so fond of. For one, it could be that my PC's just not strong enough for this game, but often during combat the controls wouldn't feel responsive and my inputs (especially dodging) would get eaten if I didn't mash them, leading to excessive rolls. Resources respawn readily, so it's not like they're super scarce, but the sheer amount of wood and ore and such you need for town upgrades ended up becoming more of a chore than a diversion after a while. Speaking of, vendors in town could use either more voice lines, less frequent voice lines, or both. Lastly, the kaizo platforming section with all the fire was a serious annoyance. Platforming with this camera angle is too imprecise to punish with instant death and long waits.

Overall, it was still a pretty excellent experience, with the level design, creature design, and art style being standouts so excellent that I feel like way more people ought to experience this game. I will definitely be coming back for the full release!
Really liked my time with this so far. The art style is great, as is the game play. Looking forward to more.

The game is great, with stunning art and addictive gameplay, but experimenting with new builds is too resource-intensive, forcing you to cheat. If you're mid-game and want to change your build, you'll likely have to recreate your character. This seems to be a weakness of most ARPGs.

But anyway, the game is still very much worth playing, and I've had over 300 hours of fantastic gameplay.

This is a souls-like Diablo and I love it. I only hesitate spending more time in the game prior to full launch since there will be a character wipe. 

Money well spent, looking forward to more adventures.
Epic and addictive gameplay, even better with friends.

amazing game was fun solo but buddy got it and its perfect now. we havnt made it very far into the story lvl20 or so so, its been so fun.  bosses arent to bad with 2 people playing, friendly fire def keeps it interesting.  9/10 just waiting on the full release now! fingers crossed its this year if its done.
A superb ARPG/souls-like crossover with amazing mechanics, engaging storylines and cool environments & world-building. Cannot wait for the 1.0 release!

Its pretty fun, the crucible boss feels pretty overtuned to me though considering i can do everything else in the game including power level 4 plagued regions without much difficulty but struggle hard with the echo knight
